255:
13.07.2022
"The Russian Liberal." Semyon Skrepetsky, acrylic on canvas, painting, 68/49 cm.
Russian liberals love to ponder freedom, democracy and human rights, but once alone they always sit on a bottle and masturbate to a bust of Lenin, imagining how they shoot all the dissenters. This canvas captures the moment when Alexei Arestovich and the Russian liberal Dmitry Bykov pine for the Soviet Union.

259:
I think on the 17th, a few hours before the march, we'll decide on a place to gather if anyone wants to join, so as not to spook the Russian opposition in advance. And then we'll join the march.

For those who want to print my icons — here's a link to the drive where the paintings are in the best possible quality.

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1-F0uY6C5Sqat2hJLHkz6E8gIFkx6CZbt?usp=sharing
260:
29.07.2022 Gavrila loved to settle in at the observatory on a starry night.
"Under the Stars." Semyon Skrepetsky, oil on canvas, 38/46 cm. Painting.
261:
03.08.2022 "Kombucha." Semyon Skrepetsky, canvas, oil, painting.
Russian news from 03.08.2022 — Muscovites bought a kombucha culture from a granny for 100 rubles and launched a beverage factory that will replace Coca-Cola.
262:
07.08.2022 "Resources." Semyon Skrepetsky, oil on canvas, painting. 68/50 cm.
From time immemorial the Russian land is full of resources, and since ancient times overseas foes have schemed to attack the dear Russian land, and plunder the great resources, and defile the Orthodox deadwood, and screw the Russian man behind the village council, turning him into a soulless gay. But the Russian man is strong and mighty and ready to fight in an unequal brawl to the last drop of fecal alcohol, defending his resources from the overseas foe.
263:
14.08.2022
"The Death-Notice Holiday." Semyon Skrepetsky, oil on canvas, painting, 100/70 cm.
The first holiday in the village — a death notice from the front; the happy parents won the lottery, their son gave his worthless life for the glory of the fatherland and now they'll receive huge compensation, enough to buy a new car. The whole village celebrates and takes pride, especially since the happy parents are pouring the drinks.
264:
21.08.2022 "The Last Supper Before the Start of the War." Semyon Skrepetsky, painting, oil on canvas, 90/60 cm.
On 21 February 2022 Vladimir Putin performed a rite of communion to war: he gathered all his supporters and gave them communion, feeding them his "flesh."
265:
9 August 2022.
"The Armed Forces of Ukraine have begun mass production of Russian 'samovars' near Kherson. Production is in full swing. Give us more Russian samovars, good ones and varied! Let the Russians rejoice and take pride!"

Painting, acrylic.
("Samovars" = limbless wounded veterans.)
266:
"Not a Sandwich."
Painting, oil on canvas. 50/36 cm.
Semyon Skrepetsky.
That very icon that turned the Navalnyites inside out at the Russian march.
Saint Not-a-Sandwich holds in his hand a sausage sandwich that is not Crimea; in his left hand a Bible with Navalny's ideology. Direct quotes.

Semyon Skrepetsky
267: Skrepetsky's paintings. A video from a collector.
268:
"The Merry Widow."
Oil painting.
Semyon Skrepetsky. Painting, oil on canvas. 110/90 cm.
On 17 November 2024, Yulia Navalnaya, together with Semyon Skrepetsky, Vladimir Kara-Murza and Ilya Yashin, held a Russian march in Berlin. The march turned out merry and lively; drunken participants yelled "The Reichstag is ours" and frightened the Germans. At the end of the march Yulia Navalnaya climbed onto a table and, as an encore, sang the song — Because I had my Alyoshka!

Semyon Skrepetsky

276:
22.08.2022
"Give Me Back My 1987." Painting, Semyon Skrepetsky, oil on canvas. 46/58 cm. For the last 10 years of his worthless life, Dmitry Medvedev drank vodka and pined for the Soviet Union. Periodically Dmitry would pull a bast shoe off his foot and, furiously banging it on the table, threaten to show everyone Kuzka's mother.
277:
01.09.2022
"Smart Voting at a Meeting of the Stools' Trade Union."
Semyon Skrepetsky (Painting, oil on canvas. 73/92 cm.
Alexei Navalny and his comrades always called for not resisting Putin's regime, convincing their followers that a fascist regime can only be defeated at the polls. Sometimes Navalny held acts of Russian revolt, and all his supporters went out into the courtyard at night, sat on a bottle and shone flashlights into the sky, frightening Putin. Later Navalny decided to become a martyr and went to the GULAG, where he organized resistance. Navalny created a secret revolutionary organization codenamed the "Stools' Trade Union." The revolutionaries continued to vote smartly and furiously shine their flashlights, thereby hastening the collapse of the fascist Putin regime.
278:
05.09.2022
"Female Skreposaurs Fight Over a Male, or — The Last Man in the Village."
Oil on canvas, painting. 80/60 cm. Semyon Skrepetsky.
The painting captures the moment of a battle of female skreposaurs over the only male. Due to Russia's rapid negative development, an acute shortage of male individuals occurred; most skreposaurs died out from excessive consumption of industrial alcohol, and some gave their worthless lives fighting for the greatness of Russia. Every male individual is a valuable unit, and the females fight fiercely over them. The painting depicts not just a male skreposaur — this is a veteran of the special operation, a hero-veteran-pensioner and accordingly an enviable groom, the dream of any she-skreposaur, so the battle will be bloody.
279:
"Patriot." Semyon Skrepetsky, painting, oil on canvas. 50/40 cm.

After the death of Alexei Navalny, the FSB released, in his name, the book "Patriot," which was an instruction manual on how to go to prison and die for the motherland, and also — how to fight totalitarian regimes with balloons and flashlights without causing those regimes any inconvenience.

Semyon Skrepetsky

294:
14.10.2022
"Comrade Musk. Through the Stars to the Thorns."
Semyon Skrepetsky, oil on canvas, painting. 50/40 cm.
Comrade Musk made a dizzying career, through thorns to the stars and back.
Elon Musk has a life principle that whatever takes off should come back down. So do his spaceships, and so did Comrade Musk himself.
295:
"Saint Maria, Savior of Veterans."
Semyon Skrepetsky, painting, oil on canvas. 60/60 cm.
Putin's daughter — Maria Vladimirovna Vorontsova — while in the special-operation zone, saved 5 wounded veterans from a burning hospital. Maria Vladimirovna carried the precious bodies of patriots on her back, dodging enemy bullets and shells! Vladimir Putin raised a worthy daughter!

"V.V. Putin's daughter is a candidate of medical sciences, a professional doctor, a well-known endocrinologist. Maria Vorontsova was with the rank-and-file soldiers at the front. She organized a mobile hospital where about 10 thousand wounded soldiers were treated. In December 2024 the mobile hospital was surrounded by the enemy and subjected to bombing… M.V. Vorontsova, together with the medical staff, carried wounded soldiers out of the railway cars; Maria herself dragged out four wounded soldiers, and while dragging out the 5th she was caught in a mine blast, received shrapnel wounds, bandaged herself, and without stopping continued providing medical aid. V.V. Putin modestly kept silent about this. After her wounds, doctor Maria Vorontsova was treated in a hospital and is now undergoing rehabilitation. Our president raised a worthy daughter."
296: "Saint Maria, Savior of Veterans." Semyon Skrepetsky, painting, oil on canvas. 60/60 cm. Putin's daughter — Maria Vladimirovna Vorontsova — while in the special-operation zone, saved 5 wounded veterans from a burning hospital. Maria Vladimirovna carried the precious bodies…
